name: Split Pea Pate
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the split peas and sort out any tiny rocks or odd-shaped peas. Put the peas in a medium saucepan with 4 cups of water.
2. Add the kombu, carrot, garlic, onion, bay leaf, and parsley to the saucepan. Cover tightly and simmer until the peas are very soft (about 35 to 40 minutes).
3. Remove the bay leaf and parsley from the mixture. Pour it into a blender in two batches and blend until smooth.
4. Return the blended mixture to the pan.
5. In a small bowl, mix 3 tbsp arrowroot with 4 tbsp water, 2 tbsp smoked yeast, 1 tsp sea salt, and 2 pinches of ground nutmeg.
6. Add these seasonings to the pea mixture in the pan. Stir until well blended.
7. Bring the mixture to a simmer, stirring constantly. When the pate detaches itself from the sides of the pan, turn off the heat.
8. If the pate seems too liquid, stir in another tablespoon of arrowroot dissolved in a tablespoon of water to make the mixture firmer.
9. Turn the pate mixture out into a lightly oiled serving dish and refrigerate. If the mixture has firmened enough, you can unmold it onto a plate and garnish with more parsley sprigs.
Variation:
In place of split peas, use 2 cups of lentils and increase the amount of water to about 5 cups. Insert 4 cloves into the carrot halves. Along with the kombu, carrots, garlic, onion, bay leaf, and parsley, add the leafy tops from 4 stalks of celery. Cook these ingredients with the lentils and water for 30 to 35 minutes. Remove the cloves from the carrots. Then puree the mixture as in the recipe above.
